Title: Sultan Nazrin launches Universiti Malaya Strategic Plan 2026-2030
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Sultan of Perak Sultan Nazrin Shah today launched the Universiti Malaya (UM) Strategic Plan 2026-2030 at the university here.
The five-year roadmap marks a new phase for UM as it seeks to build a more resilient, progressive and high-impact future for the community and the nation.
The plan is anchored on the aspiration of a resilient future shaped by learning, research and healthcare, while being strengthened through digital excellence, sustainability and a commitment to global impact.
Sultan Nazrin, who is also UM chancellor, was welcomed at the Tunku Chancellor Hall by UM vice-chancellor Prof Datuk Seri Dr Noor Azuan Abu Osman.
Also present were UM pro-chancellor Tan Sri Zainun Ali and UM board of directors chairperson Tan Sri Zarinah Anwar.
Noor Azuan said the plan was developed to ensure UM remains relevant amid technological advancement, global health challenges, sustainability demands and the increasingly complex needs of the nation.
"UM's strategic plan has been reinforced through an in-depth scenario planning process to map the future landscape by 2030.
"The mapping is also fully aligned with the Madani Economic Agenda and the Malaysian Higher Education Blueprint 2026-2035 to ensure that UM's direction is in line with national aspirations," he said.
He said the plan was a collective effort involving more than 110 university members, including the board of directors and management.
Following a nine-month phase of engagement with industry players, alumni, staff and students, the plan translates shared aspirations into a future-oriented and anticipatory document.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: There are obstacles before success, says James Chai
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: "Before success, there are obstacles that must be overcome."
These were the words political analyst James Chai shared on social media last night before appearing at the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) headquarters today.
Chai, a former aide to former economy minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli, is having his statement recorded as part of an investigation into a government-backed semiconductor project. In the same post, he also wrote the phrase: Here goes nothing.
Chai, who is based in London, had been sought by the MACC since March. The agency even offered to cover his flight costs to facilitate his return to assist in investigations into the RM1.11 billion project.
The MACC had earlier received a letter dated March 9 from his lawyer stating that Chai was unable to return due to flight costs and work commitments.
The commission responded with a follow-up letter on March 13, granting an extension until March 31. However, he failed to appear at the time.
When he was first sought, Chai criticised the agency, claiming it had created the impression he was evading authorities.
He had said that the narrative portrayed him, a former special duties (policy) officer to the then economy minister, as a fugitive.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Students free to voice views, engage govt through proper channels - Anwar
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has welcomed student criticism and activism, saying that young voices are allowed to raise issues but should be channelled through proper engagement.
Anwar was responding to Andi Mohd Asyraf Paturusi, 21, a Mara-sponsored student from Sabah and student Council representative, studying Bachelor of Computer Engineering at UCSI who noted that the landscape of student activism is no longer confined to physical spaces but is now driven largely online.
"We do not stop students from holding gatherings. If they want to criticise or raise issues, go ahead."
"If it gets coverage, we will respond." Anwar said during a question-and-answer session at the official launch of UCSI University's campus expansion.
"Student activism has changed over time particularly with the rise of social media" he said.
However, he said students also have the option of using more structured approaches to present their concerns.
"Students can organise through writing a formal letter and meeting.  The vice-chancellor could organise with the student representative of UCSI to meet the deputy minister of higher education. They will get a response within two weeks", he said.
"You can ask all the difficult questions there," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid to meet Anwar today over Negri Sembilan political crisis
Article:
CYBERJAYA: Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi will meet Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today to discuss the political crisis in Negri Sembilan.
Zahid, however, declined to disclose the timing or location of the meeting.
"Today, I cannot tell you. Even if I tell you, you still won't be able to enter," he said after attending the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry's Enforcement Day celebration and the launch of the National Consumer Policy 2.0.
Meanwhile, Anwar confirmed the planned meeting when speaking to reporters earlier today.
He said he had an audience with the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, yesterday, and Tuanku Muhriz had decreed that the state government should
continue operating
Yesterday, Negri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun said he would continue his duties as usual, adding that the state administration would function as normal in line with the decree of the Yang di-Pertuan Besar Negri Sembilan Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir.
Aminuddin said he had briefed Tuanku Muhriz on the latest developments, and the Ruler decreed that the state administration should continue until there is final clarity on the situation.
Earlier yesterday, the state's 14 Barisan Nasional state assemblymen yesterday announced they had
withdrawn support
for Aminuddin.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Don't let universities become 'certificate factories' - Anwar
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called on the Higher Education Ministry to monitor the quality of universities, warning that higher education institutions should not become mere 'certificate factories' producing low-quality qualifications.
"I urge the Higher Education Ministry to monitor and safeguard university quality. If a university does not move towards excellence, it will become lacklustre and become a certificate mill producing low-quality certificates."
"This will affect future generations. That is why we emphasise quality across all fields," he said at the Temu Anwar programme, where he also launched the new UCSI University campus expansion here.
Malaysia's position as a regional education hub, he added, makes it crucial to maintain academic standards, noting that around 160,000 international students are currently enrolled in local universities.
Also present at the event were Higher Education Deputy Minister Adam Adli Abd Halim and Anwar's wife, Bandar Tun Razak Member of Parliament Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail.
Meanwhile, Anwar also stressed the need for a balanced education system that integrates knowledge, human values and technological advancement.He said universities must play a key role in producing graduates who are not only knowledgeable, but also grounded in strong moral values, ethics and character.
Anwar added that technological progress, including artificial intelligence (AI), must not come at the expense of human development and values.
"That is why I am attracted to the approach taken by this university (UCSI) and others. While we reach for the skies in terms of technology and AI, we must not abandon our foundation."That foundation is culture, character, values and ethics, which make us unique and special.
"So we must pursue both. In our education system, while we advance in new technologies, science and AI, we must also place strong emphasis on the arts, culture, music and value-based considerations," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia property market holds steady in Q1 2026 amid global headwinds
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's property market maintained steady momentum across the residential, office, logistics and data centre segments in the first quarter of 2026 (Q1 2026), despite growing global uncertainty and rising cost pressures.
JLL managing director Jamie Tan said the market continues to be supported by resilient domestic demand, supply constraints in key asset classes and sustained investor interest, even as geopolitical tensions, elevated energy costs and supply chain risks weigh on overall sentiment.
She noted that the sector's strength lies in its diverse demand drivers, with residential remaining the largest contributor, office demand shifting towards higher-quality spaces, industrial assets benefiting from supply chain diversification, and data centres emerging as the fastest-growing segment.
Residential, which accounts for 62 per cent of total property transactions in Malaysia, remained stable in early 2026, with transaction volume declining by 1.5 per cent but transaction value rising 1.3 per cent, indicating a more selective but still liquid market.
Tan said that buyer activity also remained healthy, with Kuala Lumpur recording its strongest quarterly residential performance in four years in the fourth quarter of 2025, with up to 4,734 transactions and a transaction value of RM5.8 billion.
Johor continued to post year-on-year growth, albeit at a slower pace after two years of rapid expansion, while Penang remained stable but less aggressive, with a flatter trajectory and signs of mild cooling after a stronger 2025.
Transaction activity in Q1 was relatively muted due to the festive season, which slowed deal flow and limited price discovery in several sub-markets.
Despite this, Mukim Batu remained the Klang Valley's top-performing residential submarket, with average apartment and condominium prices at RM611 per sq ft, while service apartments in Mukim Kuala Lumpur led the market at RM758 per sq ft.
Georgetown prices normalised after a sharp rise in 2025 driven by high-end projects such as Middleton, The Light City and Skyview Residence, while in Johor Bahru, service apartment prices continued to outperform, reaching RM738 per sq ft, overtaking both the Klang Valley's RM608 per sq ft and Georgetown's RM695 per sq ft.
In the office market, Kuala Lumpur recorded a stronger occupancy, positive net absorption and higher rents across all three major submarkets in the first quarter, signalling continued recovery in occupier demand.
Occupancy in Greater Kuala Lumpur's city centre rose to 82.5 per cent in the first quarter, while KL fringe continued to lead the market at 93.5 per cent, while decentralised areas also improved, reaching 78.3 per cent.
Average achievable rents also rose across all three submarkets, with city centre offices averaging RM7.45 per sq ft per month, KL fringe at RM6.75 and decentralised locations at RM5.26.
JLL Malaysia head of office leasing advisory Quiny Lee said the market is increasingly being defined by a "flight to quality", with occupiers prioritising newer, greener and better-connected office buildings that support talent retention, operation efficiency and environmental, social and governance (ESG) targets.
Green-certified offices are outperforming older stock, commanding rental premiums of about 17 per cent. Premium Grade A green buildings are achieving average rents of RM10.47 per sq ft per month, nearly double older non-green offices at around RM5.70.
Lee added that the widening gap between premium and ageing office stock is also creating stronger pressure for landlords to upgrade older assets or risk long-term obsolescence, making asset enhancement and ESG upgrades a growing theme in 2026.
Meanwhile, the industrial and logistics sector remained firm in Q1, benefiting from the broader China-plus-X manufacturing shift with Malaysia remaining a preferred destination for supply chain diversification, logistics expansion and industrial investment.
Demand remained strongest in electrical and electronics, logistics, pharmaceuticals, automotive and medical manufacturing, with Johor seeing increased activity linked to cross-border logistics and data centre-related supply chains.
Data centres continued to stand out as the fastest-growing segment, with JLL describing Malaysia as one of Southeast Asia's most dynamic digital infrastructure markets.
Operational data centre capacity is expected to more than double from 900 megawatts at end-2025 to 2,055 megawatts by end-2026, driven by strong demand from artificial intelligence, cloud computing, 5G and big data.
Johor remains the country's primary data centre growth engine, supported by its proximity to Singapore, cost competitiveness and large-scale hyperscale development, while Greater Kuala Lumpur continues to strengthen as a secondary hub led by Cyberjaya and northern Klang Valley.
Looking ahead, JLL expects Malaysia's property market to remain cautiously resilient throughout 2026, supported by diversified demand and stronger structural fundamentals, though developers and investors are likely to remain selective amid higher costs and ongoing global volatility.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: China pledges to strengthen energy security, economic self-sufficiency
Article:
BEIJING: China's top leadership on Tuesday pledged to strengthen the nation's energy security and respond to external shocks by continuing to pursue a policy of rapid technological development and greater control over supply chains to strengthen economic self-sufficiency.
The Politburo, a top decision-making body of the ruling Communist Party, was cited as saying by state news agency Xinhua that the economy got off to a better-than-expected start this year.
The Xinhua readout did not mention the US-Israeli war on Iran, but said: "We must systematically respond to external shocks and challenges, improve energy resource security guarantee levels and counter various uncertainties with the certainty of high-quality development."
The phrase "high-quality development" refers to the pursuit of scientific and technological progress with the goal of moving China higher on the value-added ladder.
China's economy grew 5.0 per cent in the first quarter, at the top of its full-year target range of 4.5 per cent to 5.0 per cent, showing higher resilience than most other economies to the energy and commodity shocks caused by the Iran war.
Analysts say ample oil reserves, heavy use of coal and high adoption of solar, wind and electric vehicles have given China better chances of weathering the closure of the Strait of Hormuz than many European or Asian economies.
But China is not immune to the fallout from the conflict.
Soaring energy and raw materials prices threaten to drive up production costs and squeeze already thin margins at factories that employ hundreds of millions of people. And the economic hit taken by other countries could slow demand for Chinese exports.
Shipments grew just 2.5 per cent last month, slowing sharply from 21.8 per cent in the January to February period.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Unity government unaffected by political developments in NS, says Fahmi
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Negri Sembilan crisis does not affect the unity government at the federal level, government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said.
Fahmi, who is also the Communications Minister, said the government remains concerned about the political developments in the state.
"No, for now, no (it doesn't affect the relationship between parties in the unity government).
"This is a matter that may be unique to Negri Sembilan, but at the unity government's level, we are very concerned about what is happening.
"At the leadership level, we are aware that Pakatan Harapan (PH) Negri Sembilan made an announcement yesterday, and at the federal level, I believe the leadership will determine the direction," he told a press conference after launching the Google 2026 APAC Online Safety Dialogue in Malaysia here today.
Also present were his deputy, Teo Nie Ching and National Cyber Security Agency chief executive Dr Megat Zuhairy Megat Tajuddin.
As such, Fahmi said the PH Convention scheduled for May 17 at Persada Johor would provide a platform for leaders to gather feedback from grassroots members nationwide and set the coalition's direction moving forward.
"InshaAllah, on May 17, PH will hold its convention at Persada Johor, and that may serve as a good platform for the leadership to hear views from grassroots members across the states regarding the direction that should be taken," he said.
Meanwhile, when asked about a meeting between Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, who is also PH chairman, and Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Fahmi said he had no information.
He said he met Zahid yesterday morning, during which the latter shared several views that were received positively and were reassuring.
"I do not have that information.
"I was with Zahid early yesterday morning; he shared several views which I received positively, and they were reassuring.
"I am not sure whether a meeting between the prime minister and him (Zahid) has taken place," he said.
Yesterday, Umno and BN
withdrew
their support for the Negri Sembilan state government led by Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun, citing a crisis involving the state's Adat Perpatih customary system and its constitutional framework.
State Umno Liaison Committee chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias said the party leadership had taken note of developments involving the state's Adat Perpatih system and its interaction with the state constitutional framework.
However, DAP
fully supports
Aminuddin's leadership to continue administering the state, even as a minority government.
DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke had said any move to replace the menteri besar must go through the proper process, namely via a vote of no confidence in the State Assembly or upon the directive of Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir to vacate the post.
Yesterday, Anwar also
played down
the possibility of a snap state election in the state.
He said any decision to dissolve the State Legislative Assembly ultimately rests with the Yang di-Pertuan Besar, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Former Bar president calls for 'Charity Commission' amid proposed new MACC laws
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The regulatory framework governing charitable foundations requires a radical overhaul, says former Malaysian Bar president Salim Bashir.
His call aligns with ongoing discussions regarding the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission's proposed new legislative package, which includes a Political Financing Act, a Welfare Act, and a Public Office Misconduct Act aimed at tightening financial governance.
To bridge persistent systemic gaps in oversight, Salim advocated for the creation of a statutory Charity Commission to serve as a dedicated regulatory body for the sector.
"The government should consider setting up an oversight body like a Charity Commission with its own statutory powers and rules, with an intent to regulate and guide the proper management of trust and charitable foundations," he told the
New Straits Times
.
Salim emphasised that the sanctity of public trust is easily undermined when those tasked with the stewardship of these funds are perceived to be exploiting them for personal enrichment.
He warned that common "red flags" of mismanagement include a lack of financial transparency, the diversion of assets for personal benefit, and a failure to adhere to a foundation's core mandate and governing constitution.
This issue of accountability mirrors concerns raised recently by other legal experts, who noted that religious and welfare funds often fall into a
regulatory grey zone
because various enforcement bodies manage different funds.
Experts argue that current laws are often too slow to act, typically stepping in only after the money has disappeared.
This reactive approach means the "gatekeepers" tasked with protecting these funds are rarely held responsible for the loss.
Salim noted that the absence of a centralised monitoring body is a "conspicuous" gap in Malaysia's legal landscape, where regulations remain fragmented across the Federal Constitution, the Income Tax Act, the Registrar of Societies, and the Companies Commission of Malaysia.
He maintained that a dedicated regulatory body is essential to bolster institutional transparency and safeguard public funds from further mismanagement.
In light of these risks, Salim advised the public to exercise rigorous due diligence before committing assets or donations to any foundation.
He urged donors to scrutinise the backgrounds of boards of trustees and monitor the fiscal movements of the organisations they support.
"The public must remain vigilant to avoid falling prey to fraudulent entities masquerading as charitable foundations," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesia to remove import duties on some plastic products and LPG for petrochemical industry
Article:
JAKARTA: Indonesia plans to remove import duties on some plastic products as well as liquified petroleum gas (LPG) bought by the petrochemical industry to help plastic producers cope with a naphtha shortage, its chief economic minister said on Tuesday.
Coordinating Minister for Economic Affairs Airlangga Hartarto told reporters the measure will take effect in May.
Domestic prices for plastic have risen between 50 per cent to 100 per cent because of disruptions in the supply of naphtha imported from the Middle East caused by the war in Iran, Airlangga said.
It could have knock-on effects on other prices because it affects the supply of plastics used for packaging, he said.
He said the import duty will be cut to zero per cent on materials used to make plastic packaging, including polypropylene, LLDPE and High-Density Polyethylene (HDPE).
"We will give everything zero per cent import duty but only for six months," he said.
The measure is intended to dampen the increases in the prices of plastics and other products, such as food and drinks packaged in plastic, he said.
The government will reassess the situation after six months, the minister said.
Indonesia currently applies import duties of between 5.0 pe rcent and 15 per cent on plastic products.
The duty on LPG imported by the petrochemical industry will also be cut to zero per cent from 5.0 per cent, Airlangga said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia, EU in final stage of signing free trade agreement
Article:
BANDAR SERI BEGAWAN: Malaysia and the European Union (EU) are now in the final stage of signing a Free Trade Agreement (FTA) as well as finalising a comprehensive partnership aimed at strengthening economic cooperation and bilateral relations.
Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said the positive development is driven by the EU, which now views Southeast Asia as a strategic and important bloc following unilateral tariffs imposed by the United States and instability resulting from the Russia-Ukraine conflict.
Without specifying a timeline, he said Malaysia wants to finalise the agreement as soon as possible so that it can act as a catalyst to boost trade between Malaysia and the EU.
"I also mentioned this earlier during the meeting with EU representatives," he told Bernama and RTM after holding a bilateral meeting with the EU High Representative for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy, Kaja Kallas, on the sidelines of the 25th ASEAN-EU Ministerial Meeting (AEMM) here, today.
On Sept 25 last year, EU Trade Commissioner Maroš Šefčovič was reported to have said that the Malaysia-EU FTA is expected to be finalised this year, with trade between Malaysia and Europe, which reached €46 billion, expected to grow rapidly once the agreement is implemented.
Mohamad said that during the meeting with Kallas, he also raised the issue of the strong anti-palm oil campaign in Europe and rejected the ongoing anti-palm oil campaigns being actively promoted in countries in the region.
He stressed that the campaign is not based on scientific evidence but is instead driven by lobbyists from competing oil industries in neighbouring countries.
"I said that it is unfair because labelling food as containing palm oil clearly violates trade ethics," he said.
According to him, Kallas understood and agreed with the basis of Malaysia's arguments and expressed the EU's willingness to help address the unfair campaign.
Touching on the EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR), Mohamad urged the EU not to equate Malaysia's forest sustainability standards with practices in other countries.
"More than 55 per cent of our land is still covered by tropical forests, and we do not destroy forests to plant oil palm," he stressed.
He emphasised that the majority of Malaysia's palm oil industry is professionally managed by large plantation companies that fully comply with the Malaysian Sustainable Palm Oil (MSPO) standards.
In this regard, he called on the EU to conduct a detailed assessment of the sustainable management of oil palm estates in Malaysia, which are recognised as having high standards, before imposing any discriminatory measures.
Meanwhile, Mohamad said Malaysia's current focus is on exporting processed palm oil with higher added value, rather than merely exporting crude palm oil.
He said processed palm oil is not only used as cooking oil but is also widely utilised across various downstream industries, including chocolate manufacturing.
"The European Union is a major importer of processed palm oil. Therefore, this is a profitable market for us, and that is why we must firmly counter this anti-palm oil campaign," he said.
He added that with continuous clarification, Malaysia hopes the EU will better understand the actual practices in its palm oil industry and no longer protect anti-palm oil lobbyists who could affect international trade value.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Top Jalisco cartel leader 'El Jardinero' arrested in Mexico
Article:
MONTERREY: Mexican special forces have arrested Audias Flores, known as "El Jardinero" and one of the top commanders of the powerful Jalisco New Generation Cartel (CJNG), in the western state of Nayarit, security minister Omar Garcia Harfuch said on Monday.
Flores – a regional commander in control of swathes of CJNG territory along Mexico's Pacific coast – was considered a potential successor to Nemesio Oseguera, alias "El Mencho," who ran the cartel and was killed in a security operation in February.
Security forces surrounded a cabin in El Mirador, some 20 km (12 miles) north of the popular resort city of Puerto Vallarta, where Flores was being protected by a perimeter of some 30 pickup trucks and over 60 gunmen, according to a press release from Mexico's navy, which led the operation.
Flores' escorts scattered as a diversion but he was located as he tried to hide in a drainage ditch, it added.
"The operation was carried out with surgical precision without a single shot being fired," the navy said in a statement.
Videos shared by Garcia Harfuch on social media showed aerial footage of the arrest as helicopters hovered overhead during the operation, which the navy said followed 19 months of surveillance and involved more than 500 troops, six helicopters and several planes.
The navy also used intelligence provided by US authorities in the successful capture, including aerial surveillance, according to a Mexican security official who spoke on the condition of anonymity.
It was not immediately clear if Flores would face charges in Mexico, but Garcia Harfuch said he was wanted for extradition by US authorities, who have offered a US$5 million reward for his arrest.
Later in the day, Mexican authorities announced they had arrested Cesar Alejandro N., nicknamed "El Güero Conta," who Mexican authorities say was a key money launderer for Flores.
'Bigger' than El Mencho
Carlos Olivo, a former US Drug Enforcement Administration assistant special agent in charge and a CJNG expert, said Flores was a "significant figure" and that his arrest "will have a bigger effect on CJNG operations than El Mencho being taken out."
Flores was key to operations within the Jalisco cartel, controlling networks of drug laboratories, smuggling routes, and distribution networks within the US, according to the Mexican security official.
Mexico has significantly stepped up its security efforts, under enormous pressure from US President Donald Trump.
Trump has repeatedly threatened to use unilateral military action in Mexico if he feels that the Mexican government is not doing enough to fight the cartels.
Last year, the Trump administration also tied tariff threats to what it considered insufficient measures to halt fentanyl trafficking and curb migration. The US, Mexico and Canada are in the process of reviewing their shared free trade pact.
Monday's arrest of "a key leader of the violent CJNG represents a significant step forward in the fight against those who profit from fentanyl and fuel violence in our communities," US Ambassador to Mexico Ronald Johnson said on social media.
The US Treasury Department identified Flores as a "significant foreign narcotics trafficker" in 2021, when a US grand jury had charged him with conspiracy to distribute cocaine and heroin, among other crimes.
The arrest marks the latest blow to Mexican drug cartels after El Mencho's killing – a personal mission for Garcia Harfuch, who blamed El Mencho for a 2020 assassination attempt that left two of his bodyguards dead.
Flores was among several top regional leaders who security analysts believed could be well placed to take over control of the cartel.
Mexico's security situation is facing tight scrutiny as the country prepares to co-host the FIFA World Cup this summer alongside the US and Canada.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: LPI Capital Q1 profit edges up on stronger investment income
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: LPI Capital Bhd's net profit for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026, rose 1.6 per cent to RM99.5 million from RM97.98 million a year ago, supported by higher profit from the investment holding segment.
According to a bourse filing, the investment holding segment's pre-tax profit jumped 10.8 per cent to RM23.6 million from RM21.3 million previously, mainly contributed by higher tax-exempt dividend income received from its equity investment.
Revenue also grew six per cent to RM547.7 million from RM515 million last year, driven by the general insurance segment.
LPI Capital said Malaysia's economic fundamentals are expected to remain resilient, supported by steady domestic demand and continued infrastructure activity.
However, the company remains mindful of ongoing challenges, including claims volatility, medical inflation, climate-related risks and heightened geopolitical uncertainties.
"Present conflicts in the Middle East, especially the war in Iran, will continue to contribute to global
market volatility, higher energy prices, a volatile investment environment and broader economic uncertainty, which could have indirect implications for the insurance industry," it said.
LPI Capital said it will continue to expand its distribution channels to grow market share despite softer insurance demand amid a broader economic slowdown.
It noted that the agency network will be further extended in areas where LPI Group is not well-established.
Represented and the quality of agency service will be enhanced to better serve a bigger pool of customers.
The company said the partnership with Public Bank will further strengthen access to customers and long-term growth prospects.
"The LPI Group has begun deploying artificial intelligence (AI) and robotic process automation to enhance operational efficiency.
"The use of AI will be progressively extended to more core functions to further increase operational effectiveness, improve customers' experience and support sustainable business growth," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Matrade targets RM4.5bil in sales at Mihas 2026
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysia External Trade Development Corporation (Matrade) is targeting RM4.5 billion in sales at the Malaysia International Halal Showcase 2026 (Mihas 2026).
Its chief executive officer Abu Bakar Yusof said that the agency also expects the exhibition to attract at least 2,300 exhibitors from more than 50 countries at this year's event.
"Last year, our sales target was RM4.3 billion, but this year, taking into account global geopolitical developments and global crises, we are targeting to generate RM4.5 billion in overall sales," he told reporters after the pre-launch of Mihas 2026 here today.
He said Mihas 2026 will introduce a new component, Women in Export (WiEX), which combines entrepreneurial elements for women entrepreneurs and women-owned exporters to enable them to explore international markets.
In his welcoming speech, Abu Bakar said even though women-owned businesses in Malaysia make up about 20 per cent of the country's small and medium enterprises, they are still under-represented in the global market.
"WiEX turns untapped talent into export capacity by bringing high-potential companies into global markets, strengthening Malaysia's resilience by broadening its exporter base and reducing reliance on a narrow group of firms.
"It reflects Malaysia's inclusive growth model, where more capable businesses, especially women-led enterprises, are empowered to compete globally, driving greater exports of ideas, innovation and value-added products as part of Malaysia's next-generation trade strategy," he said.
Themed "Shaping Trust, Driving Resilience", Matrade said Mihas 2026 reflects the growing importance of trusted trade ecosystems in today's challenging geopolitical environment, where supply chain disruptions, market fragmentation and rising business costs continue to affect companies worldwide.
In a statement, Matrade said Mihas 2026 will take place from Sept 23 to 26, 2026, at the Malaysia International Trade and Exhibition Centre, Kuala Lumpur, combining physical and digital engagement with artificial intelligence-powered trade facilitation tools to help buyers, exhibitors and investors identify opportunities and convert engagements into measurable commercial outcomes.
"The event will also facilitate high-value business engagements through the participation of 200 international buyers under the International Sourcing Programme and 50 premium buyers under the Hosted Buyer Programme, while showcasing the strength of local industry with 600 Malaysian exporters featured through Mihas Showcase initiatives," it said.
Commenting on the event, the Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry Deputy Secretary-General (Investment and Management) Datuk Bahria Mohd Tamil said the halal sector is no longer just a contributor to growth but a strategic enabler of economic transformation, as Malaysia embarks on the 13th Malaysia Plan.
"By aligning with the New Industrial Master Plan 2030 priorities, we aim to establish Malaysia as a global halal leader, driving halal exports to RM80 billion and increasing the sector's gross domestic product contribution to 11 per cent through innovation and resilience," she said in her speech.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt to introduce national laureate-led mentorship programme for young writers
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Education Ministry will introduce a mentorship programme led by national laureates to nurture young writers and strengthen the country's literary ecosystem.
Its minister, Fadhlina Sidek, said the initiative would be carried out with Dewan Bahasa dan Pustaka (DBP) and members of the literary community.
She said the programme is important as literature reflected humanity, nurtured sympathy and empathy, and recorded the values and wisdom of life as the foundation for building national civilisation.
"In this regard, national laureates carry a major responsibility to sow human consciousness, shape identity and intellect, and strengthen cultural identity through great works that transcend time and generations, and continue to be read, studied and appreciated.
"Efforts to develop and polish writers with the potential to be recognised as national laureates must be carried out continuously and consistently.
"In this context, DBP serves as the core institution that provides the ecosystem and a clear pathway towards developing future national laureates," she told reporters at the Education Ministry today.
Fadhlina said this after announcing the late Abdul Rahman Napiah, better known as Mana Sikana, as the recipient of the 16th National Laureate Award.
She said national laureates were writers whose works reflected strong ideas, social observation and national values.
"Most of them received the recognition at a frail stage late in life, although they had been writing since a young age.
"We want to take this opportunity to ensure recognition is given when writers are producing their best works, and not only at the end of their lives," she said.
Fadhlina said this was the thinking behind the mentorship programme, which would focus on identifying and training literary talent from a younger age.
"What is most important is that their works must continue to live in society. That is why we see this programme as a very important platform.
"The government's commitment is to provide the ecosystem, guided by the wisdom and experience of the National Laureates," she added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Masidi: Sabah entitled to RM3.019bil from last year's income tax, Customs
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: Sabah is entitled to at least RM3.019 billion from federal revenue collected in the state last year, said Datuk Seri Masidi Manjun.
The Sabah finance minister said, for 2025, total collections from direct taxes under the Inland Revenue Board and indirect taxes under the Customs Department are estimated at RM7.547 billion.
"Based on this, Sabah's 40 per cent special grant entitlement amounts to approximately RM3.019 billion," he said in reply to questions during the Sabah Legislative Assembly sitting by Alias Sani (Warisan-Sekong) and Datuk Seri Jeffrey Kitingan (Star-Tambunan) on the exact amount Sabah is entitled to from federal revenue collected in the state.
Masidi, who is also deputy chief minister, said Customs duties and income tax are two categories of taxes that are clear and not in dispute, as both figures are publicly available through annual statistics.
He added that these taxes should be paid first.
"That is why we sent a letter on April 10 requesting that the federal government pay the 40 per cent share of these two undisputed taxes.
"All taxes that we believe we are entitled to, we will claim. We are still in the process of negotiation, and there are data that we cannot share at this time," he said.
Masidi said the decision not to reveal certain figures publicly is to preserve Sabah's negotiating position.
"We do not negotiate through the press, but directly with the other party. There are statistics we can share, but others remain under discussion and should not be made public as it may affect our position," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Google signs classified AI deal with Pentagon, The Information reports
Article:
WASHINGTON: Alphabet's Google joined a growing list of technology firms to sign a deal with the US Department of Defence to use its artificial intelligence models for classified work, The Information reported on Tuesday, citing a person familiar with the matter.
The agreement allows the Pentagon to use Google's AI for "any lawful government purpose", the report added, putting it alongside OpenAI and Elon Musk's xAI, which also have to supply AI models for classified use.
Classified networks are used to handle a wide range of sensitive work, including mission planning and weapons targeting.
The Pentagon signed agreements worth up to US$200 million each with major AI labs in 2025, including Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google.
The Pentagon is seeking to preserve all flexibility in defence and not be limited by warnings from the technology's creators against powering weapons with unreliable AI.
Google's agreement requires it to help in adjusting the company's AI safety settings and filters at the government's request.
The contract includes language noting "the parties agree that the AI System is not intended for, and should not be used for, domestic mass surveillance or autonomous weapons (including target selection) without appropriate human oversight and control", according to the report, but also adds that the "Agreement does not confer any right to control or veto lawful Government operational decision-making".
Reuters could not verify the report. Alphabet and the US Department of Defence, which has now been renamed the Department of War by President Donald Trump,  did not immediately respond to requests for comment.
A spokesperson for Google Public Sector, the unit that handles US government business, told The Information that the new agreement is an amendment to its existing contract.
Reuters had earlier reported that the Pentagon had been pushing top AI companies such as OpenAI and Anthropic to make their tools available on classified networks without the standard restrictions they apply to users.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: North Korea sharply increased executions during pandemic lockdown, rights group says
Article:
SEOUL: North Korea sharply raised the number of executions it conducted after shutting its borders during the Covid-19 pandemic, with the largest share linked to violations of bans on foreign culture and religion, a Seoul-based civic group said on Monday.
A report by the Transitional Justice Working Group documented 60 execution cases in which 148 people were put to death between 2020 and 2024, up from 41 executions over the previous five years.
The findings were based on interviews with 880 North Korean defectors living in South Korea and the group used satellite images to map execution sites. It cautioned, however, that the report should not be considered as definitive.
The report said breaches of restrictions on foreign culture and religion, including watching South Korean dramas and movies, accounted for the largest portion of the executions.
Before the pandemic closures, murder was the most frequent reason for an execution.
The number of executions for political crimes also rose to 28 from four in the corresponding period, the report said, as North Korean leader Kim Jong Un sought to crack down on dissent during the pandemic lockdowns.
Executions tied to cultural violations also occurred in inland areas of the country, not only in border regions with China, suggesting South Korean media content had spread throughout the country, said Hubert Lee, the group's executive director.
North Korea has slowly started reopening the country and approved in 2023 the return of its citizens who had been abroad and recently resumed passenger train service with China.
Still, the number of North Korean defectors, an indicator of North Korea's openness to the outside world, remained low at 223 in 2025, compared to 1,275 in 2015, according to South Korean government data.
The anticipated succession of Kim's daughter, known as Ju Ae, could also increase executions, said Lee.
"The number of executions may surge when the time for Ju Ae to succeed is imminent, to remove the inner circle of her father and appoint her people," said Lee.
North Korean embassies in Singapore and London and Pyongyang's permanent UN mission did not immediately respond to requests for comment on the findings of the report.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Four suspected cable thieves injured in crash while fleeing police
Article:
TUARAN: Four suspected cable thieves who were fleeing police were injured when the four-wheel-drive vehicle they were travelling in crashed into another pickup truck in Kampung Berungis, here, early this morning.
The crash, which occurred about 4.30am, also left the 61-year-old driver of the other vehicle, an Isuzu Invader, injured and trapped inside his truck.
Tuaran fire station chief Kurong Beki said the station received a distress call at 4.44am and despatched a team of eight personnel to the scene, located 9km away.
"At the scene, the fire and rescue team found that the accident involved two vehicles — a Toyota Hilux Vigo carrying four victims and an Isuzu Invader with one victim.
"Based on initial information, the Toyota Hilux Vigo was believed to have been carrying suspects in a cable theft case who were trying to escape after being chased by police.
"However, the vehicle crashed into the Isuzu Invader, causing all occupants in the Toyota Hilux and the sole occupant in the Isuzu Invader to sustain injuries.
"The fire team extricated the elderly man who was trapped inside the Isuzu Invader using special equipment," he said.
All the victims were later handed over to the medical team and taken to Tuaran Hospital by ambulance before the operation ended at 5.34am.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Jalaluddin: BN's withdrawal of support not a 'backdoor' move
Article:
SEREMBAN: Negri Sembilan Barisan Nasional (BN) assemblymen have denied that their withdrawal of support for Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun is a tebuk atap (backdoor) move to topple the state government.
Pertang assemblyman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias said the decision was made to defend party principles and prevent political interference in matters concerning the monarchy and customary institutions.
He said the move followed a crisis involving customary institutions and the state constitution, which the administration failed to resolve, leading to open debate on social media.
"The issue is the menteri besar's failure to manage affairs related to institutions, customs, and the constitution, which led to the current royal crisis.
"Whether the assembly is postponed is a separate matter. Tebuk atap means an opposition trying to become the government. We are already the government; there is no reason for us to do that," he told reporters here today.
Jalaluddin, who is the state Umno liaison committee chairman, rejected claims that the move was a pre-planned political strategy to oust the menteri besar.
He added that if BN had such intentions, the coalition could have acted much earlier.
He said that during the formation of the unity government, all parties agreed on four fundamental pillars that should remain untouched.
"You cannot touch matters related to the rulers, religion, or Malay rights. This was our agreement," he said.
Jalaluddin said the best course of action now is to dissolve the state assembly to return the mandate to the people and restore administrative stability.
"When this happens, I think it is best to return it to the people for a new mandate through the dissolution of the assembly," he said.
He added, however, that any further decision remains at the discretion of the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir.
He said the coalition would proceed through the proper legal and constitutional channels.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nestlé Malaysia posts strong Q1 FY26 profit, hits highest quarterly earnings in four years
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Nestle (Malaysia) Bhd's net profit rose to RM205.12 million in the first quarter (Q1) ended March 31, 2026, from RM161.34 million a year earlier.
This marks its highest quarterly net profit since Q1 financial year 2022 (FY22), when it recorded RM205.18 million.
The higher net profit was driven by solid topline growth, disciplined cost management and ongoing operational efficiencies across the value chain.
Revenue for the quarter increased 6.3 per cent to RM1.88 billion from RM1.77 billion.
In a statement, Nestlé Malaysia said the improvement was driven by solid topline growth, enabled by strong commercial execution and brand activations throughout the festive season, alongside disciplined cost management and ongoing operational efficiencies across the value chain.
It said this was further supported by a downward correction in the prices of key commodities such as coffee and cocoa.
"Overall, these actions underscore the company's ability to manage external cost pressures while maintaining a relentless focus on growth, with sustained investment in its brands and capabilities," it said.
Nestle Malaysia chief executive officer Juan Aranols said while the external environment in 2026 is defined by volatility and increased uncertainty, the company is confident in the strength of our fundamentals and our ability to ensure continuity in this context.
Aranols noted that its diversified portfolio of trusted brands, extensive local manufacturing footprint and broad distribution network, in combination with downstream supply chain capabilities, continue to provide a solid foundation to navigate this environment and deliver another year of resilient commercial and financial results.
"We will continue to focus on keeping our brands relevant and accessible, executing consistently across all channels, and managing costs with discipline.
"This approach allows us to maintain continuity in performance while positioning Nestle Malaysia for further sustainable, long-term growth," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Negri Sembilan Umno exco members ordered to vacate offices, return state assets
Article:
SEREMBAN: Negri Sembilan Umno has ordered its four state executive councillors to vacate their offices and return all government assets, including official vehicles.
State Umno liaison committee chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias said the decision to return the assets to the state secretary today follows the unanimous withdrawal of support by 14 Umno/Barisan Nasional (BN) assemblymen for Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun.
"When I announced that we no longer had confidence in the menteri besar and were withdrawing support for the state government, my position as an exco member was automatically forfeited.
"Today, I have directed all exco members representing Umno to surrender all facilities, including official vehicles, to the state government," he said here today.
Jalaluddin, who is Pertang assemblyman, served as the senior exco for rural development, plantation and commodities, as well as the agriculture, food security and cost of living committee.
The three other Umno representatives in the state administration are Seri Menanti assemblyman Datuk Ismail Lasim (education and human capital), Palong assemblyman Datuk Mustapha Nagoor (youth and sports development), and Linggi assemblyman Datuk Mohd Faizal Ramli (heritage, technology, innovation and digital).
Yesterday, Aminuddin confirmed the four exco vacancies following BN's withdrawal of support, which stems from a crisis involving the state's customary institutions and constitution.
Aminuddin, the Sikamat assemblyman, maintained that he would continue his duties leading a minority government and dismissed the need to
dissolve the state assembly
in the near future.
The political crisis in Negri Sembilan was triggered by the dismissal of the Undang of Sungei Ujong and issues involving the Yang di-Pertuan Besar, leading to the recent postponement of the state assembly sitting.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Train collision kills 14, injures dozens near Jakarta
Article:
JAKARTA: Indonesia's president ordered an investigation Tuesday after a long-distance train smashed into a stationary commuter train overnight, killing 14 people and injuring dozens.
Officials ended a nearly 12-hour rescue effort near Bekasi Timur station, east of the capital Jakarta, which saw crews prising open mangled carriages following the Monday night collision.
"And this morning... it is all finished," Mohammad Syafii, head of the National Search and Rescue Agency (Basarnas), told a news conference Tuesday. "I am certain there are no more victims to be found."
One survivor described the terror of being trapped inside a crushed carriage.
"I thought I was going to die," Sausan Sarifah, 29, said from her bed at the RSUD Bekasi hospital where she was admitted with a broken arm and a deep cut to one thigh.
She was on her way home from work on Monday night, she said, when her train stopped at the Bekasi Timur station some 25 kilometres (15 miles) from Jakarta.
"It all happened so fast, in a split second," Sausan recounted.
"There were two announcements from the commuter train. Everyone was ready to get off, and then suddenly there was the sound of the locomotive, really loud," she said.
"There was no time to get out, and everyone ended up piled up inside the train, crushed on top of one another. I do not know how the person underneath me is doing."
She said she had feared suffocating to death in the human pile-up, and worried that some pinned underneath did not make it.
"Thank God I was on top, so I could be evacuated quickly," said Sausan.
State-owned rail operator KAI said on Tuesday morning that the death toll had risen to 14. Another 84 people required medical treatment, it said, without specifying how many remained hospitalised.
According to Franoto Wibowo, a KAI spokesman, a taxi appears to have clipped the commuter train at a level crossing, causing it to come to a standstill on the tracks, where it was hit.
At the station, chaotic scenes unfolded in the aftermath of the crash, with rescue workers shouting for oxygen tanks as ambulances stood by in a snaking queue, lights flashing.
An AFP reporter at the scene witnessed people being carried out of the wreckage on gurneys and loaded into waiting ambulances as hundreds of bystanders looked on, some seemingly in shock.
The military, fire brigade, the national search and rescue agency, and the Red Cross aided in the massive evacuation effort that followed.
President Prabowo Subianto on Tuesday visited hospital patients in Bekasi, offered his condolences to relatives of the deceased, and said he had ordered an "immediate investigation."
He also ordered the construction of an overpass in Bekasi.
"In general, we do see that many railway crossings are not guarded," the president said.
"I have ordered that we immediately repair all these crossings, either by guard posts or by flyovers."
[[nid:1428121]]
Rescue efforts
Jakarta police chief Asep Edi Suheri said the long-distance train had crashed into the last, women-only, carriage of the commuter train.
All the victims were in the commuter train, and all 240-odd passengers on the other train had been evacuated safely, according to Anne Purba, another KAI spokeswoman.
The agency said it would cover all medical expenses for the injured and funeral costs for the deceased.
The collision had caused "significant damage to several train carriages," the Jakarta search and rescue agency said in a statement.
Several people were trapped in the carriages "due to the force of the impact," it added.
The rescue agency would not say Tuesday whether all had been freed.
Eva Chairista, 39, told AFP she had rushed to the RSUD hospital after hearing that her sister-in-law, who she named only as 27-year-old Fira, had been injured in the crash.
She arrived at a frenetic scene of medical triage.
"The doctor told us to be patient, there are many whose condition is worse than my sister-in-law's," she said.
Transport accidents are not uncommon in Indonesia, a vast archipelago nation where buses, trains and even planes are often old and poorly maintained.
The previous major train crash in the Southeast Asian country killed four crew members and injured about two dozen people elsewhere in West Java province in January 2024.
Sixteen people were killed when a commuter train crashed into a minibus at a level crossing in Jakarta in 2015.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Salesman charged with attempting to murder ex-girlfriend
Article:
BUKIT MERTAJAM: A building materials salesman was charged at the magistrate's court here today with attempting to murder his former girlfriend earlier this month.
Vincent Lim Jia Leong, 31, claimed trial to the charge which was read to him by the court interpreter before magistrate Roshayati Radellah.
He is alleged to have rammed a 41-year-old woman and a 44-year-old man with a BMW bearing registration number VER8191, with the knowledge that his actions could cause death.
The offence was allegedly committed about 9.45pm along Jalan Lorong Perusahaan Maju 8, Auto City, Prai, on April 15.
The charge was framed under Section 307 of the Penal Code, which provides for a jail term of up to seven years, or a fine, or both, upon conviction.
The prosecution was led by deputy public prosecutor Siti Nur Amalina Harun, while the accused was represented by counsel Parmjit Singh.
The court allowed bail at RM10,000 with one surety, along with an additional condition that the accused is not to disturb the victims or prosecution witnesses.
Earlier, the prosecution had proposed bail of RM15,000, but the defence sought a lower amount, citing the accused's monthly income of RM2,500 as a salesman.
The court fixed June 4 for mention.
It is understood that both victims sustained minor injuries after they were allegedly hit by the suspect, who had been trailing them from the Auto City area.
The suspect is said to have first rammed one of the victims' vehicles before they stopped to talk.
He then made a U-turn and drove into both victims before fleeing the scene.
The motive is believed to be jealousy, as the woman is the accused's former girlfriend and is said to be in a new relationship.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysian Bar: Naming a new MACC chief no 'panacea' for institutional gaps
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Bar has welcomed the appointment of Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman as the new Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission chief commissioner effective May 13.
However, it warned that the end of leadership uncertainty is not a panacea for the commission's broader institutional challenges.
It expressed concern over the government's practice of annual tenure renewals, questioning if the current framework is "fit for purpose" to command public confidence.
"The MACC must be reformed to fortify its independence through transparent appointments and a fixed term for its chief commissioner to avoid unnecessary annual speculation," said Malaysian Bar president Anand Raj.
The Bar reiterated calls from its "Memorandum for the Reform of the MACC" to insulate the commission from executive influence and secure leadership tenure to ensure true institutional independence.
Halim, a former High Court judge,
succeeds Tan Sri Azam Baki
as the leader of the nation's graft-busters.
The Bar noted his extensive experience makes him well-acquainted with foundational principles of justice, including the presumption of innocence and the right to a fair trial.
"The Malaysian Bar looks forward to him bringing this considerable knowledge to bear fully and conscientiously in the discharge of his duties," the statement added.
It emphasised that the role demands a high degree of objectivity, urging the new chief to lead "without fear or favour" and maintain conduct beyond reproach to restore public faith in the agency's mandate.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Anwar to meet Zahid over Negri Sembilan political situation today
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is expected to meet Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today to discuss recent political developments in Negri Sembilan.
Anwar confirmed the planned meeting when speaking to reporters, following an audience with the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, yesterday.
He said Tuanku Muhriz had decreed that the state government should continue operating as usual amid the ongoing situation.
Referring to moves by Barisan Nasional representatives to cooperate with Perikatan Nasional assemblymen, Anwar said all parties should adhere to the royal decree.
"Based on the royal decree, the state government should continue as it is for now, unless there is a need for change," he said.
Anwar also has urged BN in the state to prioritise unity and stability amid ongoing economic pressures, saying this is not the time for political jostling over positions.
He said efforts to resolve the political situation in the state would not be straightforward.
On when the matter is expected to be resolved, Anwar said no timeline could be set as discussions were still ongoing.
"I cannot give a date. We must allow space for all parties to negotiate properly," he said, adding that the appointment of the menteri besar involves consultations with the state's four Undangs.
Anwar was speaking to reporters after launching UCSI University's campus expansion here.
Yesterday, Umno and Barisan Nasional (BN)
withdrew their support
for the government led by Negri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun, citing a crisis involving the state's Adat Perpatih customary system and its constitutional framework.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gombak Setia rep suspended two days for defying Selangor Speaker
Article:
SHAH ALAM: Gombak Setia assemblyman Muhammad Hilman Idham has been ordered out of the Selangor state assembly and suspended for two days starting today for defying Speaker Lau Weng San.
Lau issued the order after Hilman ignored instructions to sit down following a reply by state Public Health and Environment Committee chairman Jamaliah Jamaluddin on potential state revenue from renewable energy.
The commotion began when Hilman stood up to request a supplementary question. Despite being told to sit down, the Perikatan Nasional representative refused to comply.
Lau then ordered Hilman to leave the chambers and suspended him until tomorrow.
Yesterday, two other PN assemblymen, Dr Afif Bahardin (Taman Medan) and Mohd Rafiq Mohd Abdullah (Kuang), were ordered out and suspended for three days.
Their suspension followed an attempt to move a motion to refer Wong Siew Ki (PH-Seri Kembangan) to the Rights and Privileges Committee.
The motion was linked to allegations that Wong had questioned the Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah's decree regarding a ban on pig farming in the state.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Aminuddin to stay on as Negri MB following royal decree – Anwar
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun will continue serving as Negri Sembilan Menteri Besar following a decree by the Yang di-Pertuan Besar.
Anwar said the state's political crisis is being resolved through legal channels.
"Yesterday I had an audience with the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz, and I expressed my gratitude for His Royal Highness' consideration, particularly for His request that the Menteri Besar continue his duties in the interest of the state and the welfare of the people," he told reporters after launching UCSI University's campus expansion here.
He said Aminuddin had accepted the royal decree and will continue carrying out his duties while outstanding constitutional matters are clarified.
Anwar stressed that the situation did not stem from any failure within the state government, but rather from differing interpretations of the state constitution involving traditional institutions.
"The problem does not arise from the state government or the Menteri Besar. It stems from a legal interpretation involving the state constitution and the Undangs and the Yang di-Pertuan Besar. That is not under our jurisdiction," he said.
He added that the government respected the legal framework and the role of the monarchy.
"For us, we respect the state laws and at this stage we recognise the role, sovereignty and dignity of the royal institution," he said.
Anwar said efforts would continue to ensure the matter is resolved amicably, including consultations involving the relevant parties in Negri Sembilan.
"To clarify the situation, the Menteri Besar will need to engage in discussions with the Undangs. What happens next depends on the outcome of those discussions," he said.
He cautioned against any premature political conclusions that could destabilise the state administration.
"It is not fair to make rushed decisions that could create political instability in Negri Sembilan," he said, adding that priority must be given to the state's stability and public welfare.
Anwar also emphasised the importance of handling issues involving royal institutions with care and respect.
"Matters involving the institution of the Rulers must be handled with wisdom, with care, and in a way that preserves its dignity and sanctity. That is what we want," he said.
Yesterday, Anwar played down the possibility of a
snap state election
in the state following the leadership crisis.
Anwar said any decision to dissolve the Negri Sembilan state legislative assembly ultimately rests with the Yang di-Pertuan Besar.
"Not likely (for a snap election). It depends because it is up to the Tuanku (Muhriz) and, of course, the assembly sitting," Anwar said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 31 nabbed in four ops as several scam syndicates crippled in Selangor
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have crippled several scam syndicates operating out of Selangor following four coordinated raids between April 14 and 24, leading to the arrest of 31 individuals and the seizure of equipment worth about RM160,000.
Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said the operations, carried out by the Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID) with assistance from district police headquarters, targeted call centre-style scam operations, online gambling fraud and investment scams.
In the first raid about 10pm on April 14, a team from the Ampang Jaya district police CCID stormed a house in Taman Ampang Utama.
Police arrested 10 foreigners — eight men and two women — aged between 23 and 41, believed to be involved in receiving and converting money suspected to be proceeds of scams.
"Investigations found the syndicate had been operating since January with one suspect acting as a cook while the others served as operators managing funds from overseas, including from the Philippines, Taiwan, Hong Kong, Indonesia and India," Shazeli told reporters today
He said transactions were conducted via Telegram before being converted into cryptocurrency (USDT) and returned to the syndicate's handlers.
Police seized four laptops, 15 mobile phones, an iPad and a router, with total items valued at about RM20,000.
Shazeli said in a second operation on April 20 about 6.45pm, police raided a unit at Brezza One Residency, Bukit Ampang Permai, and arrested 10 suspects — a local man and nine foreigners aged between 18 and 33.
"The group was believed to be running an online gambling scam targeting Chinese citizens.
"They acted as operators promoting fake gambling schemes via Tencent and Telegram, promising winnings to victims. Payments were made through Alipay and WeChat Pay, but the gambling platform did not exist," he said.
Police seized seven monitors, seven CPUs, 21 mobile phones and a router, estimated to be worth RM60,000.
Shazeli said the third raid on April 21 about 2.30pm saw police from the Selangor CCID headquarters detain eight local men at a condominium in Petaling Jaya.
"All suspects, aged between 26 and 38, were believed to be involved in managing layered bank transactions linked to scam proceeds.
"They acted as account managers, facilitating fund transfers across multiple bank accounts, including up to four layers involving nine local bank accounts," he said.
Police seized eight computers, eight laptops, 19 mobile phones and a router, with items valued at RM40,000.
All eight have since been charged under Section 417 and Section 120B of the Penal Code and fined RM5,000 each.
Shazeli said in the fourth operation on April 24 about noon, police raided a house in Sunway Geo Avenue, Subang Jaya, and arrested three men, one local and two foreigners aged between 21 and 32, for their involvement in an online investment scam targeting Chinese nationals.
"They acted as operators promoting bogus investment schemes through a website known as Omnissa Horizon Client," he said.
Police seized 10 mobile phones, five laptops, three monitors, a router and a modem, with an estimated value of RM40,000.
"Across all four operations, investigations revealed the syndicates began operating between December 2025 and February this year, with members playing roles ranging from operators and account handlers to logistical support," said Shazeli.
All cases are being investigated under Section 420 and Section 120B of the Penal Code, with several suspects also remanded under the Immigration Act 1959/63.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pantai Jerejak assemblyman's trial for allegedly hurting wife set for Aug 18
Article:
BALIK PULAU: The magistrate's court here today fixed Aug 18 for the trial of Pantai Jerejak assemblyman Fahmi Zainol, who is charged with voluntarily causing hurt to his wife in February.
Magistrate Chia Huey Ting also set May 14 for the submission of documents, including the medical report.
Defence counsel Dr Shamsher Singh Thind pressed for the matter to proceed without further delay, saying an earlier two-month deferment had already been excessive.
"A further two-month postponement, even for mention, would only prolong the case unnecessarily," he told the court.
Shamsher told the court the complainant had lodged two police reports — before and after the incident — and that there had been attempts to withdraw the report, stressing that the complainant remained a key prosecution witness.
"As such, any move to retract the report must be treated with caution," he added.
Shamsher described the case as a domestic dispute and said an expeditious resolution could benefit both parties.
"It would be easier for the husband and wife to reconcile if the matter is resolved quickly," he said, while applying for the earliest available dates, noting the case did not involve serious or complex issues requiring higher-level intervention.
Deputy public prosecutor Ikmal Affendi Zulkifly informed the court that the medical report is expected to be ready within the week.
The court maintained May 14 for document submission and Aug 18 for trial, directing the prosecution to furnish all available documents in the interim.
Earlier, Fahmi, a former state executive councillor for agrotechnology, food security and cooperative development, arrived at the Balik Pulau court complex with his wife about 8.50am.
On Feb 27, he was charged at the same court with voluntarily causing hurt to his wife, Nur Hidayah Abd Jabar, 35, and pleaded not guilty.
The offence was allegedly committed at about 10pm on Feb 22 at a house along Persiaran Pantai Jerejak 11 in Bayan Lepas.
The
charge
is framed under Section 323 of the Penal Code, read together with Section 326A.
Bail was set at RM2,000 with one surety.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No deal after 30 years: Penang CM confirms talks failed before Waterfall Cafe demolition
Article:
GEORGE TOWN, April 28 — Talks between the Penang government and Waterfall Cafe have failed to produce a settlement, bringing to an end a decades-long dispute that has persisted since 1990, Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said today.
He said the state had repeatedly engaged all relevant parties in an effort to reach a fair and practical solution, but all negotiation channels had ultimately failed.
“In principle, all active negotiation channels that were opened failed to reach a resolution,” he said in a statement.
He said all notices directing the premises to be vacated, issued by the Northeast district land administrator, including an eviction notice on January 4, 2024 were not complied with.
“Despite outstanding rental arrears, the state government through the Northeast District and Land Office (PDTTL) offered several reasonable options to Waterfall Cafe to relocate and continue operations,” he said.
He said the relocation offer included two units at the multi-storey car park building, the commercial plaza, for the future Penang Hill cable car project, as well as a temporary site throughout the construction period.
He said the cafe owners rejected the offers without clear justification.
“It should also be noted that during a coordination meeting on June 19 in 2023 chaired by the then Deputy Chief Minister II P. Ramasamy, it was agreed that Waterfall Cafe would relocate and settle its outstanding rental arrears,” he pointed out.
He said the commitment was not fulfilled.
Chow said the first eviction notice was issued on January 4 in 2024 and is followed by several rounds of discussions from February 19 in 2024.
“The final notice was issued on April 15 this year before enforcement action was carried out today,” he said.
The cafe, located near the entrance of the Botanic Gardens, was demolished by the authorities earlier today.

Title: As scams hit RM2.9b, Fahmi warns of AI-powered fraud threats, calls for stronger action
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — Malaysians have lost about RM2.9 billion to online scams, a figure Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil today described as a serious and escalating national threat.
Speaking at the Google 2026 APAC Online Safety Dialogue, Fahmi said the scale of losses showed the urgent need for stronger coordinated action between the government, tech companies, and regional partners such as Asean.
“We need better tools to ensure the safety of our citizens.
“I look forward to more constructive engagement between NSRC and Google to address how the changing landscape of bad actors can be tackled using Google’s tools,” he said.
The NSRC refers to the National Scam Response Centre.
He said the money lost to scams could have been channelled into public development, including schools and hospitals.
Fahmi praised Google’s efforts in tackling online fraud and said Malaysia is open to deeper collaboration with technology firms and the NSRC.
He also stressed that digital access alone is not enough, noting that Malaysia’s near-universal connectivity must be matched with stronger online safety safeguards.
Beyond prevention, he said greater attention should be given to supporting scam victims through education and psychological assistance.
Fahmi warned that scams are becoming increasingly sophisticated, particularly with the use of artificial intelligence, which allows criminals to adapt quickly and evade detection.
He added that the cross-border nature of scams requires tighter inter-agency cooperation to effectively combat the threat.

Title: Johor Regent meets DPM Fadillah, MB to discuss water supply, energy sustainability
Article:
JOHOR BARU, April 28 — Tunku Mahkota Ismail, the Regent of Johor, received a courtesy call from Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Fadillah Yusof and state leaders today to discuss issues related to water supply and energy sustainability in the state.
In a Facebook post today, the Johor Regent said the meeting involved the deputy prime minister, who is also energy transition and water transformation minister, Fadillah; Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi; and Johor State Secretary Datuk Mohammed Ridha Abd Kadir.
Among the matters discussed were efforts to ensure a more systematic water supply system and sustainable electricity generation to guarantee sufficient resources for Johor’s future development needs.

Title: Muhyiddin’s trial: Mamfor’s RM19.5m ‘donations’ in 29 cheques to Bersatu flagged as suspicious, unusual to give money to political party, banker says
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — Mamfor Sdn Bhd’s payment of RM19.5 million in frequent and large transactions in 2021 and 2022 as “donations” to Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia was unusual, and all 29 cheques used to pay the money was flagged internally as suspicious transactions, the High Court heard today.
Bank Muamalat’s PKNS Shah Alam assistant branch manager Norazeanti Miswan said this while testifying as the ninth prosecution witness in Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin’s corruption and money laundering trial.
Norazeanti said her bank branch had internally reported each of Mamfor’s 29 Bank Muamalat cheques, due to the “large and frequent withdrawals paid to” Bersatu.
“Yes, each transaction carried out, totalling 29, each of the transactions had a questionable activity report or internal suspicious transaction report that was sent to Bank Muamalat’s headquarters for further evaluation, as there was doubts on each transaction carried out by Mamfor Sdn Bhd,” she said.
Norazeanti explained that this was because each of the cheques ranged from RM500,000 to RM2 million, and as the transactions were continuously made from 2021 to 2022 to the same recipient.
Responding to lead prosecutor Datuk Wan Shaharuddin Wan Ladin, Norazeanti — who has been a banker for 16 years — said the RM19.5 million payments by Mamfor was unusual for a company “because I don’t see other companies doing such transactions.”
“Because normally, other companies will may payments in line with the nature of business of that company. I don’t see other companies paying to other political parties lah,” she said.
Earlier, Norazeanti said Mamfor’s nature of business was “construction”, but it had paid the RM19.5 million to Bersatu which is a political party.
Based on banking documents, Norazeanti said Mamfor had only stated “donation” as the purpose of paying the RM19.5 million to Bersatu, confirming that the company did not use the words “political donation”.
Asked by Wan Shaharuddin, she confirmed that Mamfor had only donated to Bersatu and did not make donations to other political parties, such as Umno, PAS and DAP.
She said Mamfor had closed its Bank Muamalat account on February 26 last year.
In this trial, Muhyiddin is facing seven charges, namely four counts of alleged power abuse to obtain RM225.3m bribes for Bersatu (from Nepturis Sdn Bhd, Azman Yusoff, Bukhary Equity Sdn Bhd, and RM19.5 million from Mamfor Sdn Bhd) and three counts of alleged money laundering through money that Bersatu received from Bukhary Equity.
The trial is being heard before High Court judge Noor Ruwena Md Nurdin.

Title: LHDN ‘killing the golden goose’? Lim Guan Eng questions tax surge, urges aid for struggling MSMEs
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — Lim Guan Eng has questioned whether Malaysia’s rising tax collection is putting pressure on businesses, warning that overburdening companies could undermine long-term revenue growth.
In a Facebook post today, the former finance minister pointed to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s announcement that the Inland Revenue Board of Malaysia (LHDN) collected a record RM204 billion in direct taxes in 2025 — a 10.4 per cent increase year-on-year — and asked if such gains risk “killing the golden goose”.
He argued that the jump in tax collection appears disproportionate to Malaysia’s economic growth, noting that gross domestic product (GDP) expanded by just 5.2 per cent in the same year.
“If LHDN can increase its tax collection by 10.4 per cent, then the natural conclusion is that business profitability must have grown at a similar pace. However, this is not the case,” he said.
Lim claimed many businesses — particularly micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) — are facing declining revenues and profits, with only selected sectors such as banking performing strongly.
He warned that without thriving businesses generating income, the government’s ability to sustain tax revenue would be at risk.
